2013, Berkeley, California The Tenth Biennial Ptolemy Miniconference was held on Thursday, November 7, 2013 in Berkeley, California. The Presentations are available. The Ptolemy project studies modeling, simulation, and design of concurrent, real-ti

ing, simulation, and design of concurrent, real-time, embedded systems. The focus is on assembly of concurrent components. The Ptolemy Miniconference is an opportunity for research collaborators and Ptolemy users and extenders from industry, academia

tolemy users and extenders from industry, academia, and government to get together, present their work to the Ptolemy community, and hear about related research and results. It is typically held every two years. In addition, the meeting acted as an a

two years. In addition, the meeting acted as an annual meeting for the Center for Hybrid and Embedded Software Systems . Please direct questions to cxh@eecs dot berkeley dot edu Contact Update Help Copyright © 1999 - UC Regents; all rights reserved
